pages.title: Landing Page
pages.description: Public marketing landing page for OpenBat — 'Google Analytics for AI chatbots'. Features hero section with value proposition, animated chat widget demo, logo carousel of supported AI providers, feature sections (conversation analysis, sentiment scoring, topic detection, workflow automation, SDK integration), pricing tiers (Free through Enterprise), FAQ accordion, testimonials, and CTA sections.
elements: link: Footer links (Features, Pricing, FAQs, Docs, GitHub, Blog)

flows.name: Navigate to login
flows.description: Access the authentication page to log in to an existing OpenBat account from the public landing page.
flows.persona: Returning user who already has an OpenBat account and wants to access their chatbot analytics dashboard.
preconditions: User is not already logged in
steps: Navigate to the landing page at /
Click the 'Login' link in the top-right navigation header
User is redirected to /auth/login
flows.expectedOutcome: User lands on the login page with email/password form and social auth options.
failureModes: Page may not load if server is down

flows.name: Navigate to sign up
flows.description: Start the account creation process from the landing page to begin using OpenBat for chatbot analytics.
flows.persona: New user who has learned about OpenBat and wants to create a free account.
preconditions: User does not have an existing account
steps: Navigate to the landing page at /
Click the 'Sign Up' button in the top-right navigation header
User is redirected to /auth/sign-up
flows.expectedOutcome: User lands on the sign-up page with email, password, and confirm password fields.
failureModes: Page may not load if server is down

flows.name: Explore features via anchor links
flows.description: Scroll through the landing page sections to learn about OpenBat's capabilities before signing up, using in-page anchor navigation.
flows.persona: Prospective user evaluating OpenBat against alternatives, wanting to understand features and pricing.
preconditions: User is on the landing page
steps: Click 'Features' in the header nav to scroll to the features section
Review the feature cards: conversation analysis, sentiment scoring, topic detection, workflow automation
Click 'Pricing' to scroll to the pricing grid
Compare the 6 pricing tiers (Free, Starter, Pro, Growth, Business, Enterprise)
Click 'FAQs' to scroll to the FAQ accordion section
Expand FAQ items by clicking on them
flows.expectedOutcome: User has reviewed all marketing content and can make an informed decision about signing up.
failureModes: Anchor scroll may not work with JavaScript disabled

pages.title: Login Page
pages.description: Authentication page where existing users log in to their OpenBat account. Features a split layout with a login form on the left and a decorative image on the right. Supports email/password login and social authentication via Apple, Google, and Meta. Includes a link to the forgot password flow.

flows.name: Email login
flows.description: Authenticate using email and password credentials to access the main dashboard. This is the primary authentication method for existing users.
flows.persona: Returning user who already has an account and wants to access their chatbot analytics dashboard.
preconditions: User is not already logged in
User knows their email and password
steps: Navigate to /auth/login
Enter email address in the Email text field
Enter password in the Password text field
Click the 'Login' button to submit credentials
Wait for authentication to complete and redirect to the dashboard
flows.expectedOutcome: User is redirected to /platform showing their organization's chatbot list.
failureModes: Invalid email or password shows an error

flows.name: Social auth login
flows.description: Authenticate using a third-party identity provider (Apple, Google, or Meta) for passwordless login.
flows.persona: User who prefers OAuth-based login over managing a separate password.
preconditions: User has an account linked to the chosen social provider
User is not already logged in
steps: Navigate to /auth/login
Click one of the social auth buttons (Apple, Google, or Meta)
Complete the OAuth flow in the provider's popup/redirect
Wait for authentication to complete and redirect to the dashboard
flows.expectedOutcome: User is redirected to /platform showing their organization's chatbot list.
failureModes: Social provider OAuth popup may be blocked
Account not linked to chosen provider

flows.name: Navigate to forgot password
flows.description: Start the password reset process when a user has forgotten their credentials.
flows.persona: User who cannot remember their password and needs to reset it.
preconditions: User knows their email but forgot their password
steps: Navigate to /auth/login
Click the 'Forgot your password?' link next to the Password field
User is redirected to /auth/forgot-password
flows.expectedOutcome: User lands on the password reset page with an email input field.

pages.title: Sign Up Page
pages.description: Account creation page for new users. Features a split layout with a registration form on the left and a decorative image on the right. Requires email, password, and password confirmation. Also supports social sign-up via Apple, Google, and Meta. Includes a link to sign in for existing users.

flows.name: Email sign up
flows.description: Create a new OpenBat account using email and password. After sign-up, the user's organization is auto-created on first visit to the platform.
flows.persona: New user who has decided to try OpenBat and wants to create an account to start analyzing their chatbot conversations.
preconditions: User does not have an existing account
User has a valid email address
steps: Navigate to /auth/sign-up
Enter email address in the Email field
Enter a password (at least 8 characters) in the Password field
Re-enter the same password in the Confirm Password field
Click the 'Create Account' button
Wait for account creation and redirect
flows.expectedOutcome: Account is created. User is redirected to /platform where their organization is auto-created (named after their email prefix). They see an empty chatbot list and can create their first chatbot.
failureModes: Email already in use shows an error

pages.title: Forgot Password Page
pages.description: Password reset page where users enter their email to receive a reset link. Simple form with email input and submit button.

flows.name: Request password reset
flows.description: Submit an email address to receive a password reset link, allowing the user to regain access to their account.
flows.persona: Existing user who forgot their password and needs to reset it.
steps: Navigate to /auth/forgot-password (or click 'Forgot your password?' from the login page)
Enter the account email address in the Email field
Click 'Send reset email'
Check email inbox for the reset link
flows.expectedOutcome: A password reset email is sent to the provided address with a link to set a new password.
failureModes: Email not found in the system

pages.title: Platform Dashboard — Chatbot List
pages.description: The main authenticated landing page showing all chatbots belonging to the user's organization. Displays chatbot cards with name, API key prefix, creator, creation date, conversation count, and message count. Supports filtering by status (Active, Pending, Archive) and switching between card and list views. Each chatbot card has a context menu (three-dot button) with Open chatbot, Archive, and Delete actions.

flows.name: Create new chatbot
flows.description: Add a new chatbot to the organization to begin capturing and analyzing conversations from a new AI assistant deployment.
flows.persona: Product or engineering team member setting up OpenBat tracking for a new or existing AI chatbot.
steps: Navigate to /platform
Click the '+ New chatbot' button in the top right
Fill in the chatbot creation form (name, etc.)
Submit the form to create the chatbot
Copy the generated API key
Complete onboarding steps
flows.expectedOutcome: A new chatbot is created with an API key. User is directed to the onboarding page to set up the SDK.
failureModes: Plan limit on number of chatbots reached

flows.name: Open chatbot dashboard
flows.description: Navigate into a specific chatbot's analytics dashboard to view conversation data, sentiment trends, and insights.
flows.persona: Team member who wants to review the analytics and health of a specific chatbot.
preconditions: At least one chatbot exists in the organization
steps: Navigate to /platform
Click on a chatbot card to navigate to its dashboard
Alternatively, click the three-dot menu and select 'Open chatbot'
flows.expectedOutcome: User is navigated to /platform/[chatbotId] showing the chatbot's overview dashboard with stats, sentiment charts, and insights.
failureModes: Chatbot may redirect to onboarding if not yet set up

flows.name: Switch chatbot view mode
flows.description: Toggle between card view (visual cards with stats) and list view (compact table) for the chatbot list.
flows.persona: User with many chatbots who prefers a denser layout.
steps: Navigate to /platform
Click the 'List view' or 'Card view' toggle button in the top right area
flows.expectedOutcome: The chatbot list switches between card grid and table list layouts.

flows.name: Filter chatbots by status
flows.description: Filter the chatbot list to show only Active, Pending, or Archived chatbots.
flows.persona: User managing multiple chatbots who wants to focus on a specific group.
steps: Navigate to /platform
Click one of the status filter buttons: Active, Pending, or Archive
flows.expectedOutcome: The chatbot list filters to show only chatbots matching the selected status.

pages.title: Organization Members
pages.description: Manage team members who have access to the organization. Displays a table of members with their email, role (Owner, etc.), and join date. Includes an 'Invite member' button to add new team members.

flows.name: Invite team member
flows.description: Add a new member to the organization so they can access chatbot dashboards and analytics alongside the team.
flows.persona: Organization owner or admin who wants to give a colleague access to OpenBat.
steps: Navigate to /platform/members
Click the '+ Invite member' button
Enter the new member's email address in the invitation form
Select a role for the new member
Submit the invitation
flows.expectedOutcome: An invitation is sent to the provided email. The member appears in the table once they accept.
failureModes: Plan limit on team members reached

pages.title: Organization Settings
pages.description: Manage organization-level settings including the organization display name and a danger zone for permanently deleting the organization and all its data.

flows.name: Rename organization
flows.description: Change the display name of the organization that appears throughout the platform.
flows.persona: Organization owner who wants to update the organization name (e.g., after a company rebrand).
steps: Navigate to /platform/settings
Clear the Organization name field and type the new name
Click the 'Save' button
flows.expectedOutcome: Organization name is updated and reflected across the platform UI.

flows.name: Delete organization
flows.description: Permanently delete the organization and all associated chatbots, conversations, messages, and analytics data. This is an irreversible destructive action.
flows.persona: Organization owner who wants to completely remove their OpenBat account and all data.
steps: Navigate to /platform/settings
Scroll to the 'Danger zone' section
Click the 'Delete organization' button
Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
flows.expectedOutcome: The organization, all chatbots, conversations, messages, and analytics are permanently deleted. User is redirected to a logged-out or empty state.

pages.title: Chatbot Overview Dashboard
pages.description: Main analytics dashboard for a specific chatbot. Shows stat cards (Conversations, Messages, Avg Sentiment, Analyzed Today), date range picker, segment filter (by Plan), and two tabs: User Insights and Assistant Performance. User Insights tab includes: sentiment trend chart, top user frustrations table, most dissatisfied users with investigate links, support topics table, revenue signals (churn risk, buying signals, capability gaps), top intents chart, sentiment by segment, activity heatmap, and languages breakdown. Assistant Performance tab includes: behavior alerts table (Hallucinating, Dodging, Verbose, Redirecting with severity), resolution outcomes donut chart, quality flags table, and capability gaps table.
elements: link: sidebar nav (Dashboard, Workflows, Conversations, Deep Search, Organizations, Users, Analysis Config, Settings, Get Help)

flows.name: View user insights
flows.description: Review the User Insights tab to understand customer sentiment trends, frustration patterns, support topics, revenue signals, and user behavior over time.
flows.persona: Product manager or CS lead wanting to understand how users feel about their chatbot and identify actionable patterns.
preconditions: Chatbot has received conversations with analyzed messages
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]
The User Insights tab is selected by default
Review the sentiment trend chart to see satisfaction over time
Check 'Top user frustrations' for recurring pain points
Review 'Most dissatisfied users' and click 'Investigate' to see their conversations
Scroll to 'Support topics' to see what users are talking about
Check 'Revenue signals' for churn risk and buying signal indicators
Review 'Top intents' to understand what users are asking about
flows.expectedOutcome: User has a comprehensive view of customer sentiment and can identify areas needing attention.
failureModes: No data available if no conversations have been analyzed yet

flows.name: View assistant performance
flows.description: Review the Assistant Performance tab to identify problematic bot behaviors, resolution rates, quality issues, and capability gaps.
flows.persona: Engineering lead or bot prompt engineer who wants to improve chatbot response quality.
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]
Click the 'Assistant Performance' tab
Review 'Behavior alerts' for problematic behaviors (hallucinating, dodging, verbose, redirecting) with severity levels
Check the 'Resolution outcomes' donut chart for resolution rates
Review 'Quality flags' for issues like hallucination risk and sensitive data exposure
Check 'Capability gaps' for unmet feature requests
flows.expectedOutcome: User understands bot behavior patterns and can prioritize prompt improvements.
failureModes: No assistant analysis data if assistant message analysis is disabled in settings

flows.name: Investigate dissatisfied user
flows.description: Click through from the 'Most dissatisfied users' section to view all conversations for a specific unhappy user, filtered by their email.
flows.persona: Customer success manager who wants to understand why a specific user is dissatisfied and take action.
preconditions: Dashboard shows dissatisfied users with analyzed sentiment
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]
Scroll to 'Most dissatisfied users' section in User Insights tab
Click the 'Investigate →' link next to the user's name
User is navigated to the conversations page filtered by that user's email
flows.expectedOutcome: Conversations page shows only conversations from the selected user, allowing the team to review their interactions.
failureModes: No conversations found for the user in the selected date range

flows.name: Filter by date range
flows.description: Change the date range for all dashboard analytics to focus on a specific time period.
flows.persona: Any dashboard user who wants to analyze data for a specific week, month, or custom range.
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]
Click the date range button (shows 'This month' by default)
Select a predefined range or pick custom dates
Dashboard data refreshes for the selected period
flows.expectedOutcome: All dashboard widgets update to show data for the selected date range.
failureModes: No data available for the selected period

pages.title: Workflows
pages.description: Workflow automation page for creating alert and action workflows that trigger based on conversation analysis conditions. Has three tabs: Workflows (list of configured workflows), Runs (execution history), and Templates (pre-built workflow templates). Shows empty state with 'Create workflow' button when no workflows exist.

flows.name: Create workflow
flows.description: Set up an automated workflow that triggers alerts or actions when specific analysis conditions are met (e.g., negative sentiment detected, churn risk flagged).
flows.persona: CS lead or ops manager who wants automated alerts when important patterns are detected in chatbot conversations.
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/workflows
Click the '+ Create workflow' button
Configure trigger conditions (e.g., sentiment threshold, specific behavior detected)
Set up actions (e.g., Slack alert, email notification)
Save the workflow
flows.expectedOutcome: A new workflow is created and will automatically trigger when its conditions are met during message analysis.
failureModes: Plan limit on number of workflows reached

pages.title: Conversations List
pages.description: Paginated table of all conversations captured by this chatbot. Columns: User, Email, Organization, Messages, Sentiment (with colored badge and score), Language (flag), Plan, Last Message, Created. Supports text search, column sorting, filter button, view configuration, date range selection, and pagination (20 rows per page). Clicking a row navigates to the conversation detail page.
elements: pagination: rows per page, page navigation

flows.name: Browse conversations
flows.description: Browse, search, and filter all captured chatbot conversations to find specific interactions or patterns.
flows.persona: Team member reviewing recent conversations to understand user behavior and chatbot performance.
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/conversations
Browse the conversation table sorted by most recent
Optionally type in the search box to filter by user name, email, or organization
Click column headers to sort by Messages, Sentiment, Last Message, etc.
Use pagination controls to browse through pages
Click on a conversation row to view its detail
flows.expectedOutcome: User can find and navigate to specific conversations for detailed review.

pages.title: Conversation Detail
pages.description: Detailed view of a single conversation showing a chat-bubble interface with user and assistant messages in chronological order. Each message has timestamp, and analyzed messages show behavior/intent/sentiment badges (e.g., 'Dodging', 'Deflected', 'Helpful', 'conversational greeting'). Highlighted text spans within messages indicate detected patterns. Right sidebar shows: Overall Sentiment card, Conversation metadata (Messages, Conv ID, First/Last message, Created), User info (Name, Email, User ID), Organization info (Name, Org ID), Session info (Referrer, Device), Custom Metadata. Breadcrumb navigation back to conversations list. Filter and View buttons available.
elements: button: message bubbles (clickable for analysis details)
button: behavior badges (Dodging, Deflected, Helpful, etc.)

flows.name: Review conversation analysis
flows.description: Read through a full conversation and examine the AI-generated analysis badges on each message to understand user sentiment, assistant behavior, and conversation outcomes.
flows.persona: CS manager or prompt engineer investigating a specific conversation flagged by the system or reviewing a dissatisfied user's interaction.
preconditions: Conversation exists and has been analyzed
steps: Navigate to the conversation detail page from the conversations list or an 'Investigate' link
Read through user and assistant messages in the chat bubble view
Review colored badges on messages: behavior tags (Dodging, Helpful), resolution tags (Deflected, Partially Resolved), intent tags
Click on highlighted text spans to see details of detected patterns
Check the right sidebar for overall sentiment, conversation metadata, user info, and organization context
Use breadcrumb to navigate back to the conversations list
flows.expectedOutcome: User has a complete understanding of the conversation including what the user asked, how the bot responded, and what the AI analysis detected.
failureModes: Analysis not yet complete (badges won't appear)
No sentiment data if analysis is disabled

pages.title: Deep Search
pages.description: Semantic search page that finds conversations by meaning rather than keywords. Features a search input with a placeholder example ('users asking about pricing' or 'billing issues'), a date range picker, and a 'Deep Search' button. Shows an empty state when no query has been entered.

flows.name: Semantic search conversations
flows.description: Search for conversations using natural language queries that match by semantic meaning, not just keywords. For example, searching 'users frustrated with billing' would find conversations about payment issues even if the word 'billing' wasn't used.
flows.persona: Product manager or CS lead investigating a specific theme across all conversations without knowing the exact keywords users used.
preconditions: Chatbot has captured and analyzed conversations
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/deep-search
Optionally set a date range using the date picker
Type a natural language query in the search box (e.g., 'users asking about pricing' or 'complaints about response time')
Click the 'Deep Search' button
Review the semantically matched conversation results
flows.expectedOutcome: A list of conversations matching the semantic intent of the query is displayed, ranked by relevance.
failureModes: Query too vague returns too many results

pages.title: Client Organizations
pages.description: Paginated table of customer organizations that have interacted with the chatbot. Columns: Organization, Plan (demo/client), Industry, MRR, Conversations, Users, Sentiment (with colored badge), Last Activity. Supports text search, filtering, view configuration, column sorting, and date range selection. 48 rows with pagination.

flows.name: Browse client organizations
flows.description: View all customer organizations that have used the chatbot, sorted and filtered to understand which organizations are most active, most dissatisfied, or generate the most conversations.
flows.persona: Customer success manager wanting to identify high-risk or high-value client organizations.
preconditions: Chatbot has captured conversations with organization metadata
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/organizations
Browse the organizations table
Sort by Sentiment to find the most dissatisfied organizations
Sort by Conversations or Users to find the most active organizations
Use the search box to find a specific organization
Click on an organization row to see its details
flows.expectedOutcome: User can identify which client organizations need attention based on sentiment, activity, and engagement data.
failureModes: No organization data if conversations don't include organization metadata

pages.title: Chatbot Users
pages.description: Paginated table of individual users who have interacted with the chatbot. Columns: User, Email, Organization, Plan, MRR, Conversations, Sentiment (with colored badge and score), Last Activity. Supports text search, filtering, view configuration, column sorting, and date range selection. 26 rows with pagination.

flows.name: Browse chatbot users
flows.description: View all individual users who have conversed with the chatbot, with their aggregate sentiment scores and conversation counts, to identify specific users who need attention.
flows.persona: Customer success manager or support lead wanting to identify individual users who are struggling or highly engaged.
preconditions: Chatbot has captured conversations with user metadata
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/users
Browse the users table
Sort by Sentiment to find the most dissatisfied users
Sort by Conversations to find the most active users
Use the search box to find a specific user by name or email
Click on a user row to see their details or conversations
flows.expectedOutcome: User can identify which individual chatbot users need follow-up based on sentiment and engagement data.
failureModes: No user data if conversations don't include user metadata

pages.title: Analysis Config
pages.description: Configuration page for how chatbot conversations are analyzed. Has five tabs: Metadata (managed fields and discovered fields with accept/deny/track controls), Translation, User Analysis, Assistant Analysis, and Prompts. The Metadata tab shows managed fields table (Key, Type, Status, Used, Discovered, Actions) and discovered fields table (Key, Sample value, Occurrences) with '+ Track' buttons to start tracking new metadata keys.

flows.name: Manage metadata fields
flows.description: Accept, deny, or track custom metadata keys sent by the SDK to control which fields appear as filters in the conversations table.
flows.persona: Developer or data team member configuring which SDK metadata fields are visible and filterable in the dashboard.
preconditions: SDK is sending conversations with custom metadata
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/analysis-config
The Metadata tab is selected by default
Review 'Managed fields' to see currently tracked metadata keys and their status (Accepted/Denied)
Review 'Discovered fields' for new metadata keys found in conversations
Click '+ Track' on a discovered field to start managing it
Click the X button on a managed field to remove it
flows.expectedOutcome: Metadata fields are configured, and accepted fields become available as filters in the conversations and other tables.
failureModes: No discovered fields if SDK isn't sending custom metadata

flows.name: Configure analysis settings
flows.description: Switch between the five configuration tabs (Metadata, Translation, User Analysis, Assistant Analysis, Prompts) to fine-tune how the AI analyzes conversations.
flows.persona: Technical user who wants to customize analysis behavior — for example, adjusting prompts, enabling translation, or modifying which user/assistant analysis signals are detected.
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/analysis-config
Click through the tabs: Metadata, Translation, User Analysis, Assistant Analysis, Prompts
Configure settings in each tab as needed
flows.expectedOutcome: Analysis configuration is updated and future message analysis uses the new settings.

pages.title: Chatbot Settings
pages.description: Comprehensive chatbot configuration page with six tabs: General (chatbot name, ID, creation date, message analysis toggles, delete chatbot), Company Info, API Keys, Members, Webhooks, Import/Export Data. The General tab includes toggles for enabling/disabling user and assistant message analysis, and a danger zone for permanently deleting the chatbot.

flows.name: Rename chatbot
flows.description: Change the display name of a chatbot that appears throughout the dashboard.
flows.persona: Team member who wants to rename a chatbot for clarity (e.g., from 'test' to 'Production Support Bot').
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/settings
The General tab is selected by default
Clear the Chatbot name field and type the new name
Click the 'Save' button
flows.expectedOutcome: Chatbot name is updated and reflected throughout the dashboard.

flows.name: Toggle message analysis
flows.description: Enable or disable automatic analysis for user messages and/or assistant messages to control which signals are extracted.
flows.persona: Developer or admin who wants to disable assistant analysis to reduce costs or focus only on user sentiment.
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/settings
Scroll to the 'Message analysis' section
Toggle the 'User message analysis' switch to enable/disable user analysis (sentiment, intent, topics, flags)
Toggle the 'Assistant message analysis' switch to enable/disable assistant analysis (behavior, outcome, quality, flags)
flows.expectedOutcome: Future messages are analyzed (or not) according to the toggle settings.

flows.name: Delete chatbot
flows.description: Permanently delete a chatbot and all its associated conversations, messages, and analytics data. This is irreversible.
flows.persona: Admin who needs to remove a test or decommissioned chatbot.
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/settings
Scroll to the 'Danger zone' section at the bottom
Click the 'Delete chatbot' button
Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
flows.expectedOutcome: The chatbot and all its data are permanently deleted. User is redirected to the chatbot list at /platform.

flows.name: Manage API keys
flows.description: View, create, or rotate API keys used by the SDK to send conversation data to this chatbot.
flows.persona: Developer integrating the OpenBat SDK who needs to copy or rotate the API key.
steps: Navigate to /platform/[chatbotId]/settings
Click the 'API Keys' tab
View existing API key prefix
Rotate the key if needed
flows.expectedOutcome: API key is visible (prefix only) and can be rotated for security.
failureModes: Rotating key invalidates the old key immediately

pages.title: Chatbot Onboarding
pages.description: SDK setup instructions page shown after creating a new chatbot. Guides the user through installing and configuring the OpenBat SDK. If the chatbot is already onboarded (settings.onboarded = true), this page redirects back to the chatbot dashboard.

flows.name: Complete chatbot onboarding
flows.description: Follow the SDK setup instructions to integrate OpenBat with an AI chatbot, then mark onboarding as complete to access the full dashboard.
flows.persona: Developer who just created a new chatbot and needs to integrate the SDK into their application.
preconditions: A new chatbot has just been created
Chatbot has not been onboarded yet
steps: After creating a chatbot, user is redirected to /platform/[chatbotId]/onboarding
Copy the API key shown on the page
Follow the SDK installation instructions (npm install, code snippet integration)
Test the integration by sending a test message
Mark onboarding as complete
User is redirected to the chatbot dashboard
flows.expectedOutcome: SDK is integrated with the user's chatbot application. Future conversations are captured and analyzed automatically. User sees the full dashboard.
